[INFO] Out-of-scope components found in ds-patterns.jsx
  Source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-patterns.jsx
  Found: ds-patterns.jsx defines FIVE pattern components — TimelineSection,
    StepperSection, FileUploadZone, NotificationSection (Notification Inbox),
    FilterBarSection. The phase manifest scoped only FileUploadZone for Phase 26.
  Impact: Timeline, Stepper, Notification Inbox, and Filter Bar are NOT in any
    current phase plan. Specs for them were extracted into context.md but no
    REQ-* requirements were created for them.
  Recommendation: Add Timeline, Stepper, Notification Inbox, and Filter Bar to a
    future ingest manifest with explicit phase mapping before scheduling those
    builds. Without manifest scoping, the roadmapper will not route them.

[INFO] Two notification-pattern visual contracts coexist in handoff
  Source: ds-notifications.jsx (NotificationCenter — Phase 25 target)
    + ds-patterns.jsx (NotificationSection / Notification Inbox — out of scope)
  Found: Both files define a notification-list pattern, but with different visual
    contracts:
      - ds-notifications.jsx: 380px panel, .ds-notif-item class, success/warning/
        error/info type taxonomy, per-item dismiss, "Mark all read", InlineBanner
        sibling variant
      - ds-patterns.jsx: 440px max-width inbox, interview/update/reminder/doc
        type taxonomy, per-item click-to-mark-read, no individual dismiss,
        amber-tinted unread row background
  Impact: Phase 25 commits to the ds-notifications.jsx contract per the manifest.
    The ds-patterns.jsx Notification Inbox is a SEPARATE component pattern that
    overlaps in purpose. Building both would create two notification components
    in the library with different APIs.
  Recommendation: Before any future ingest pulls ds-patterns.jsx Notification
    Inbox into scope, decide whether it is a distinct pattern (e.g. inline
    embedded list vs. dropdown panel) or a duplicate to deprecate. This is a
    product decision, not a synthesis decision.

[INFO] StatusPage uniformity caveat — ServerErrorPage is always-dark
  Source: /Users/temp/Documents/workspace/design-system/design_handoff/design-system/ds-status.jsx
    + DECISION-004 (always-dark surfaces — prior ingest)
  Found: Of the four StatusPages, three (NotFound, Maintenance, Offline) flip
    with theme via standard --cream / --ink tokens. ServerErrorPage hardcodes
    #1c1917 bg + #f5f3f0 text per DECISION-004's always-dark rule.
  Result: Auto-resolved via existing DECISION-004. Logged as CONSTRAINT-020 in
    constraints.md. Component plans (Phase 27) must document this distinction
    so the team does not accidentally add var(--cream)/var(--ink) tokens to
    ServerErrorPage during dark-mode work.

[INFO] StatusPage parameterization deferred to component plans
  Source: ds-status.jsx
  Found: Status pages in the SPEC use hardcoded copy (incident IDs, headlines,
    countdown values, pending-change lists, etc.). For production reuse, each
    page must accept overridable props.
  Result: Logged as CONSTRAINT-021 in constraints.md. The ingest establishes
    the SCOPE of customization (which props need to be parameterized); the
    exact API contract is a per-component decision to be locked in Phase 27
    component plans, NOT in this ingest. This matches the doc-synthesizer's
    principle: synthesis preserves intent, plans pin contracts.

[INFO] FileUploadZone validation behavior unspecified
  Source: ds-patterns.jsx
  Found: The drop zone hint line says "PDF, DOCX, MD · Max 10MB" but the SPEC
    does NOT specify validation behavior — what happens when a too-large or
    wrong-type file is dropped is not defined.
  Result: Logged as CONSTRAINT-019 in constraints.md. Recommended approach
    (reject + onError callback) is documented but is non-binding. The Phase 26
    component plan must lock validation behavior before build. This is a
    routine API gap, not a conflict — flagged INFO to ensure the Phase 26
    planner addresses it explicitly.
